attributes.action: Abolish all religious exemptions for carrying bladed articles in public
attributes.additional_details:
UK law bans carrying a knife in public without a "good reason," but Section 139(5)(b) grants an exemption for religious reasons.
We believe public safety laws must apply equally to everyone, without exception. No one should carry a blade in public based on faith. The government must remove this exemption entirely to ensure one uniform standard that protects everyone.
attributes.background: Repeal Section 139(5)(b) of the Criminal Justice Act 1988 to completely remove the statutory defense that allows individuals to carry a knife or bladed article in a public place for religious reasons.

rejection.details: You may wish to sign this petition, which calls for similar action: “Ban carrying religious, cultural & ceremonial bladed & pointed items in public” https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/770412
